skill.name: ckm-design
skill.description: Design skill for logos, brand identity systems, corporate identity mockups, slides, banners, icons, and social media image assets.
skill.summary: Create brand, logo, presentation, banner, icon, and social image assets with guided design workflows.
skill.author: nextlevelbuilder

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a critical heuristic combination, but manual review did not confirm malicious intent, prompt injection, credential exfiltration, C2 behavior, or real weak cryptography. The skill has legitimate elevated behaviors: local scripts, file writes, .env credential loading, Gemini API calls, logo image upload, generated HTML, and generated SVG output. Publish with warnings about external API use and inspection of generated files.

medium_findings.title: Gemini API and Environment Credential Use
medium_findings.description: The logo, CIP, and icon generators load API keys from local environment files and use Google Gemini clients. This is legitimate for generation, but it exposes credentials and prompts to an external service. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE risk factor, legitimate use pattern.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly reads .env files and initializes Gemini clients. Intent appears functional, not malicious, because requests go to the documented generation provider.

medium_findings.title: Local Logo Images Can Be Sent to Gemini
medium_findings.description: The CIP generator loads a user-supplied logo image and includes it in Gemini generation contents. This can disclose client or unreleased brand assets to an external API. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE risk factor, consent-sensitive.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The image load and API contents path are explicit. The risk depends on user consent and asset sensitivity, so this is medium severity rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Unescaped User Input in Generated HTML
medium_findings.description: The CIP renderer interpolates brand and industry values directly into an HTML document and writes it to disk. Malicious input could create script or markup injection in the generated file. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The HTML is assembled with f-strings and no escaping is visible at the cited locations. Exploitability is limited to generated local files or hosted copies.

medium_findings.title: Generated SVG Written Without Sanitization
medium_findings.description: The icon generator extracts model-produced SVG and writes it directly to files. Generated SVG can contain active content or external references if the model output violates instructions. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script asks the model for safe SVG, but it does not sanitize or validate the final SVG before writing. This is a common generated-asset safety risk.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Directed Local Command Workflows
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run local Python scripts and headless browser screenshot commands. This is expected for the skill, but it increases execution and filesystem risk. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE risk factor, expected behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible workflow instructions, not hidden execution. Risk is elevated because agents may run them with user-provided paths and prompts.

low_findings.title: Static Keyword Blockers Are Mostly Design Vocabulary False Positives
low_findings.description: Many weak crypto, C2, reconnaissance, and certificate-key hits appear to come from design words, CSV vocabulary, examples, or HTML snippets rather than security logic. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE category summary.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed implementation uses BM25 search, design references, image generation, and HTML templates. I did not find cryptographic implementations or command-and-control behavior.

low_findings.title: Windows SAM Finding Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The critical Windows SAM finding points to formatted text for a color system in the CIP search output, not access to a Windows credential database. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines format color and typography labels for a design brief. There is no file path or database access related to Windows SAM.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Evidence Found
low_findings.description: Targeted review did not find instructions claiming system authority, pre-approval, skipped review, or override language in the inspected skill files. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for injection concern.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Keyword review and manual inspection found normal skill instructions and scope notes. This is evidence of absence for reviewed files, not a proof for every token.

dangerous_patterns.title: Automatic .env Loading from Skill and Claude Paths
dangerous_patterns.description: Generation scripts load .env files from the skill directory and ~/.claude locations. This is convenient but broadens credential exposure inside the process. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE pattern.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The paths and environment assignment are explicit. The code does not overwrite existing environment variables, which reduces but does not remove risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Gemini Generation Calls
dangerous_patterns.description: Scripts call Gemini models with prompts, generated content requests, and optional image inputs. This creates expected network egress to a third-party provider. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE pattern.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The API calls are direct and central to the skill. The provider is documented by the skill, so the concern is disclosure and governance rather than hidden exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unescaped User Input in Generated HTML
dangerous_patterns.description: The CIP renderer interpolates brand and industry values directly into an HTML document and writes it to disk. Malicious input could create script or markup injection in the generated file. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The HTML is assembled with f-strings and no escaping is visible at the cited locations. Exploitability is limited to generated local files or hosted copies.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated SVG Written Without Sanitization
dangerous_patterns.description: The icon generator extracts model-produced SVG and writes it directly to files. Generated SVG can contain active content or external references if the model output violates instructions. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script asks the model for safe SVG, but it does not sanitize or validate the final SVG before writing. This is a common generated-asset safety risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Headless Browser and Local File Screenshot Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: Reference instructions recommend Chrome headless screenshots of local HTML files. This is normal for export, but browser execution can load remote fonts or images. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE pattern, documentation-driven.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instructions are examples and references, not executable code. Risk depends on whether an agent follows them against untrusted HTML.

content.value_statement: Design teams need consistent assets across logos, presentations, mockups, banners, icons, and social platforms. This skill provides guided workflows, references, and generators for those design outputs.
actual_capabilities: Routes design requests to built-in logo, CIP, slide, banner, social photo, and icon workflows.
Searches local CSV references for logo styles, colors, industries, CIP deliverables, and mockup contexts.
Generates logo and CIP image prompts for Gemini image models when API credentials are available.
Creates SVG icon prompts and writes generated icon files for single, batch, and size variants.
Builds HTML presentation pages from generated CIP mockup images.
Documents platform sizes and screenshot workflows for banners and social media images.
limitations: Gemini generation requires a valid GEMINI_API_KEY or GOOGLE_API_KEY.
Some workflows depend on external skills that may not be installed in every environment.
Generated assets need human review for brand accuracy, text quality, and legal suitability.
The skill does not directly publish social posts or manage campaign scheduling.

use_cases.title: Launch a Startup Brand Kit
use_cases.description: Generate logo directions, core identity mockups, presentation assets, and social launch images from one brand brief.

use_cases.title: Prepare Client Design Options
use_cases.description: Create multiple logo, icon, banner, and CIP concepts for fast client review and iteration.

use_cases.title: Standardize Marketing Visuals
use_cases.description: Use platform size guides and HTML screenshot workflows to produce consistent campaign graphics.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Logo Direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a logo direction for a modern healthcare startup named ClearPath. Include style, colors, symbols, and a generation prompt.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Brand Asset Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a brand asset plan for a fintech product. Include logo style, color palette, CIP deliverables, banners, and social image sizes.

prompt_templates.title: Generate CIP Mockup Prompts
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare CIP mockup prompts for a consulting brand. Include business card, letterhead, office signage, apparel, and vehicle branding.

prompt_templates.title: Produce Campaign Visual System
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a visual system for a product launch across LinkedIn, Instagram, YouTube, web banners, icons, and a pitch deck.

output_examples.input: Create logo options for a premium coffee brand.
output_examples.output: A concise design brief with recommended styles, color palettes, typography direction, visual symbols, and generation-ready prompt language.

output_examples.input: Create social launch graphics for a SaaS feature release.
Create a CIP package for a consulting firm.
output_examples.output: Recommended deliverables, mockup contexts, image generation prompts, and an HTML presentation structure for client review.

best_practices: Provide brand name, audience, industry, tone, platform, and required formats before generation.
Review generated images, SVGs, and HTML before using them in production or client delivery.
Use local output directories and avoid sending confidential brand files to external APIs without approval.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated logos or icons as final trademarks without legal and brand review.
Do not upload confidential client assets to Gemini unless the client approved that workflow.
Do not open generated SVG or HTML files from untrusted prompts without inspecting them first.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: Logo, CIP, and icon generation need Gemini credentials. Reference searches and design planning can work without generation.

faq.question: Can it create final production artwork?
faq.answer: It can create strong drafts and mockups. Final artwork should be reviewed and refined by a designer.

faq.question: Which asset types are covered?
faq.answer: It covers logos, CIP mockups, banners, slides, SVG icons, and social media image workflows.

faq.question: Does it publish to social platforms?
faq.answer: No. It creates image assets and guidance only. It does not post, schedule, or manage accounts.

faq.question: Can it use an existing logo?
faq.answer: Yes. The CIP generator can use a local logo image, but that image is sent to Gemini for editing.

faq.question: Is generated SVG safe to use directly?
faq.answer: Inspect generated SVG first. The script asks for clean SVG, but model output is still untrusted content.
